messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoáş·c má»™t sá»‘ nguyĂŞn Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) Ä‘Ăˇnh giĂˇ Ä‘á»™ khĂł
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: "Session will stop" notification is not correct for agent host
ISSUE BODY: VS Code shows the "session will stop" notification for the agent host when closing a window, but this isn't quite right for the agent host lifecycle.

- Quitting vscode entirely- local AH sessions will stop, remote ones will not
- Closing a vscode window- local AH sessions will not stop

We might also think through whether it's confusing that a local AH session can keep running even when no window is open with that session. But the session may be open on a remote.

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Instructions  
1. **Always read the `sessions` skill first.** This is your primary source of truth for the sessions architecture.
- Invoke `skill: "sessions"`.
2. Focus your work on `src/vs/sessions/`.
3. Avoid making changes to core VS Code files (`src/vs/workbench/`, `src/vs/platform/`, etc.) unless absolutely necessary for the agents window functionality.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\instructions\agentHostTesting.instructions.md =====  
--- description: Architecture documentation for VS Code AI Customization view. Use when working in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/aiCustomization` applyTo: 'src/vs/platform/agentHost/**'
---

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Spawn-Time Env Binding  
The agent host inherits its env vars at fork time. `IAgentHostOTelService` reads `process.env` once in its constructor and caches the resolved config. Changing a `chat.agentHost.otel.*` setting at runtime therefore has **no effect** on the currently-running agent host â€” the host must respawn (reload window / restart VS Code) to pick up the new value. This is the same model used by the rest of the agent host service surface.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\sessions\browser\parts\mobile\contributions\AGENTS.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Connection Management  
- `setConnection(connection, defaultDirectory?)` â€” Wires a live agent host connection directly; dynamically discovers session types from the host's root state agents
- `clearConnection()` â€” Clears the connection when the host disconnects
- Handles session notifications (`notify/sessionAdded`, `notify/sessionRemoved`) and state changes
- Fires `onDidChangeSessionTypes` when the host's agent list changes
- Remote-host management options do not expose an IPC output channel; remote diagnostics use the host's forwarded logs when available.
- SSH connection progress notifications are closed when the connect promise settles; keyboard-interactive prompt cancellation rejects the connect promise as cancellation and does not show an error notification.
- SSH config host connections use resolved `IdentityFile` and `IdentityAgent` values from `ssh -G`; encrypted private keys are prompted for a passphrase through the same quick-input bridge as keyboard-interactive auth.
- Startup SSH auto-reconnect treats keyboard-interactive cancellation as an intentional pause and does not schedule another reconnect attempt.
- A manual SSH reconnect from the host picker bypasses that paused auto-reconnect state and starts a fresh reconnect attempt for stored SSH hosts; host-picker disconnect/cancel for SSH uses the SSH service instead of removing the stored host.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Differences from `CopilotChatSessionsProvider`  
- **No `IAgentSessionsService` dependency.** Uses `IChatService` directly.
- **No untitledâ†’committed URI swap.** Local session resources never change.
- **Multi-chat hierarchy stored in provider metadata.** A local session may host multiple chats; the parentâ†’child link is persisted via `IStoredLocalSession.parentUri` (see Multi-Chat Support above).
- **Self-managed session list.** Storage owns the source of truth, not the chat history.
- **No worktree / branch / isolation.** Local sessions run in-process against the workspace folder as-is.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\sessions\contrib\providers\remoteAgentHost\REMOTE_AGENT_HOST_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix incorrect 'session will stop' notifications for Agent Host sessions",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "sessions", "notification", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As an agent host user, I want accurate session lifecycle notifications when closing VS Code windows or quitting entirely, so that I understand whether my Agent Host sessions will continue running.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a local Agent Host session is active When the user quits VS Code entirely Then show notification indicating local AH session WILL stop",
    "Given a local Agent Host session is active When the user closes a VS Code window (without quitting) Then show notification indicating local AH session will NOT stop",
    "Given a remote Agent Host session is active When the user quits VS Code entirely Then show notification indicating remote AH session will NOT stop",
    "The notification should distinguish between 'local' and 'remote' agent host sessions",
    "The notification message should be clear about whether the session continues after window close vs. quitting"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review Agent Host session management code to understand local vs remote detection logic",
      "Identify where session lifecycle events are tracked (sessionAdded/sessionRemoved)",
      "Add metadata or flags to distinguish local AH sessions from remote AH sessions in the session model",
      "Update session provider to expose connection type information"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Locate the notification system that displays 'session will stop' messages",
      "Modify notification logic to check agent host connection type before showing stop warning",
      "Add conditional rendering for different notification messages based on local/remote status",
      "Update UI to show session type indicator in notifications"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test local AH session + quit VS Code scenario - verify 'will stop' notification",
      "Test local AH session + close window scenario - verify 'will not stop' notification",
      "Test remote AH session + quit VS Code scenario - verify 'will not stop' notification",
      "Verify no false positive notifications for unrelated sessions"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by senior engineer",
    "All acceptance criteria tests passed",
    "No regressions in existing session management functionality",
    "Documentation updated to reflect new notification behavior"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Should the notification also indicate which specific session is affected?",
    "Is there a preference for inline vs. toast notifications for this message?",
    "Should we add a dismiss button that doesn't actually stop the session?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "Agent Host sessions have a clear way to determine if they are local or remote",
    "The current notification system can be extended without breaking existing functionality",
    "Session type information is available at the time notifications are triggered"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Connection Management section",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Differences from CopilotChatSessionsProvider",
    "agentHostTesting.instructions.md - Architecture documentation"
  ]
}
```
